prompt: Using ProteinMPNN with multi-state design, generate sequences for the HIV-1 gp41 N-heptad repeat (NHR) domain in both the pre-hairpin intermediate (extended coiled-coil, PDB 1AIK) and post-fusion (6HB, PDB 2ZII) conformations to investigate the energy landscape of viral membrane fusion as an antiviral target. outline a computational approach covering: (1) superimposition of NHR core residues across pre-hairpin and 6HB backbones, (2) multi-state ProteinMPNN run to identify sequences that traverse both states, (3) coarse-grained MD to estimate kinetic barrier between states, (4) circular dichroism at varying temperatures for conformational preference validation. Discuss implications for designing fusion-inhibitor peptides that trap intermediate states.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (1AIK, 2ZII); target: gp41 NHR; organism: Human immunodeficiency virus 1; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: HIV-1 fusion protein six-helix bundle forming domain
